channel.title: 简报 Jae Daily · 2026年07月05日
channel.description: 国际政治 · 宏观财经 · 前沿科技 · AI 辅助分析

item.title: [未译] No-gift policy for Taylor Swift, but how much should you give at a wedding?
item.description: Wedding lists are being replaced by cash requests, but guests are divided over how much to give.

item.title: [未译] Large crowds gather in Tehran on first day of Ayatollah Khamenei's funeral
item.description: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla until Monday, before travelling across Iran and Iraq.

item.title: [未译] How the 1986 Mexico World Cup was almost cancelled after a devastating earthquake
item.description: Guardian reports after the disaster told of 5,000 deaths, much of the capital being razed, and doubts about Mexico hosting the finalsMexico last hosted the World Cup in 1986, but the competition was a

item.title: [未译] The Middle East is in ruins. Who will rebuild it?
item.description: From Gaza to Sudan, Libya to Yemen, and Syria to Lebanon, hospitals, schools, housing, and electricity and water networks have been destroyed by Israeli belligerence, the Iran war and civil conflicts.

item.title: [未译] The Download: a smoking “endgame” and a new Elizabeth Bear story
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The UK’s generational tobacco ban might not work.

item.title: [未译] Germany’s Second Chance for Growth Rebound Starts Now
item.description: German data in the coming week showing the cumulative impact of the Iran war will set the stage for the government’s latest bid to awaken animal spirits in Europe’s biggest economy.

item.title: [未译] 'Start work at 11' - but will other bosses be as flexible over England's 1am match?
item.description: Employers are being urged to use their "common sense" to allow staff to work flexibly where they can.

item.title: [未译] Bloomberg This Weekend 07/04/2026
item.description: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by

item.title: [未译] US Flyover Showcases Next-Generation Airpower
item.description: Retired US Army Colonel Wayne Sanders joins Bloomberg This Weekend during the 4th of July Naval Review and tall ships parade, 'Sail4th 250' taking place in the Hudson River. Col. Sanders explains to

item.title: [未译] Brazil’s Durigan Says Credit Lines Won’t Affect Monetary Policy
item.description: Credit measures introduced by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva’s government do not undermine monetary policy, Finance Minister Dario Durigan said in an interview with local news website G1 publishe

item.title: [未译] Moore: American Dream Still Worth Fighting For
item.description: Maryland Gov. Wes Moore said patriotism should unite rather than divide Americans, arguing that service and sacrifice—not politics—define love of country and remain essential to fulfilling

item.title: [未译] Coney Island Boardwalk Set for $1 Billion Upgrade
item.description: The executive director of the Alliance for Coney Island Daniel Murphy joins Lisa Mateo of Bloomberg This Weekend to explain how Coney Island is entering a new phase of growth with a permanent business

item.title: [未译] Former chess champion Kramnik banned over cheating remarks
item.description: The former Russian world chess champion had accused other players, including the late Daniel Naroditsky, of cheating.

item.title: [未译] Alibaba reportedly bans employees from using Claude Code
item.description: Alibaba has reportedly classified Claude Code as high-risk software.

item.title: [未译] What is Mistral AI? Everything to know about the OpenAI competitor
item.description: Mistral AI, which offers some open source AI models, has raised significant funding since its creation in 2023, with the ambition to “put frontier AI in the hands of everyone.”

item.title: [未译] Why California’s carbon manure math doesn’t add up
item.description: Something stinks in California’s climate policies. Years ago, the state set up a system that pays cattle farmers across the country to turn the methane emitted from cattle manure into natural gas, enc

item.title: [未译] Injury ends Williams' Wimbledon comeback
item.description: Serena Williams pulls out of her planned Wimbledon doubles appearance with older sister Venus because of a knee injury.

item.title: [未译] Pope urges Europe to do more for migrants as he visits gateway island
item.description: The pontiff pays tribute to migrants who lost their lives at sea at a cemetery on the Italian island of Lampedusa.

item.title: [未译] What should a personal website be?
item.description: <p><a href="https://lobste.rs/s/4tiool/what_should_personal_website_be">Comments</a></p>

item.title: [未译] Instagram running ads promoting child sexual abuse material in India, BBC finds
item.description: The ads use terms including “rape” and “child video” and link to content on the messaging app Telegram.

item.title: [未译] Burnham says there is some room for movement on tax
item.description: But the likely next PM says he will stick to Labour's pledges to not raise VAT, income tax or national insurance.

item.title: [未译] 'Less than 10% sheep': How millions may have unknowingly eaten goat, skin and fat kebabs
item.description: Millions are likely to have eaten "lamb" kebabs that were actually made with goat, skin and fat.

item.title: [未译] Tackle workplace sickness to unlock hidden growth, former John Lewis boss says
item.description: More than 250 of the UK's biggest employers have signed up to his 'Get Britain Working' taskforce.

item.title: [未译] Rocket Report: Indian startup nears first launch; SpaceX's millenary milestone
item.description: NASA awarded Rocket Lab deals for three dedicated launches using the company's Electron rocket.

item.title: [未译] Why Some Banks Still Charge High Overdraft Fees
item.description: Congress scrapped a rule last year that capped how much banks could charge if you spent more than was in your account. Now, banks’ revenue from the fees is rising.

item.title: [未译] Row after Indian state drops eggs from school lunch menu
item.description: West Bengal's move to replace eggs in some school meals has sparked a wider debate over nutrition and choice.

item.title: [未译] A device that revives eyeballs from dead donors could make eye transplants possible
item.description: It’s not easy to transplant a whole human eye. The surgery is difficult. And the eyes themselves start to degenerate as soon as they’ve left the body. When surgeons attempted it a few years ago, the n

item.title: [未译] AI is 'not smart' so what's next in artificial intelligence?
item.description: Leading AI researcher Yan LeCun has a start-up which is developing a more flexible AI system.

item.title: [未译] The UK’s generational tobacco ban might not work. I’m supporting it anyway.
item.description: As the parent of two little girls, I often think about how their childhood is different from mine. The seven-year-old is learning about AI at school. The five-year-old is given internet-based homework

item.title: [未译] Teaching AI to run with the turbines
item.description: Artificial intelligence may have captured the public imagination through chatbots and image generators, but some of its most consequential use cases are unfolding far from consumer-facing tools. In in
